text: Last spring, my friend Sara dragged me to the city art museum.
I had never been particularly keen on paintings, and I expected to be bored.
The first few rooms were filled with landscapes and portraits that meant little to me.
Then we turned a corner and I encountered a large painting of a storm at sea.
The colours were intense — dark blues and greens crashing against each other.
I could almost feel the wind.
I stood there for several minutes, unable to move.
Something about it triggered an emotion I could not name.
Sara noticed my reaction and smiled.
She said art does not need to be logical to be meaningful.
That single painting transformed my afternoon into something truly memorable.
I have been back to the museum three times since then, each visit revealing subtle details I missed before.
